scheme_name: Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (Scheduled Caste Category)
page_title: Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (Scheduled Caste Category)

details: The scheme "Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (Scheduled Caste Category)" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, is designed to support livestock owners during the critical post-parturition period, one month after delivery. This time is crucial for the health of the mother cattle and the calf. Under the scheme, 150 kg of BIS Type II concentrate feed is provided free of cost to livestock owners belonging to the Scheduled Caste Category.
benefits: Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits
150 kg BIS type II Concentrate feed per beneficiary
eligibility: The applicant must be a Scheduled Caste (SC) livestock owner.
The applicant must possess the following valid documents:
Government-approved photo ID (e.g., Aadhaar, Voter ID).
Caste certificate issued by a competent authority.
Certificate of calving issued by a Government Livestock Inspector.
Disability certificate from a competent authority (only if the applicant is disabled).

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at I-Khedut Portal:
On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Animal Husbandry Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button and an application page will open.
Click on "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a print out of the confirmed application.
Applicant can also check their Application Status/View/Print/Upload Application details from the below link:
Required Documents can also be submitted in hard copies to the office mentioned in the application form within 7 days.

documents_required: Certificate of calving of an animal from Govt. Livestock inspector.
Disability certificate from competent authority (If beneficiary is disabled).

faqs.question: What is the main objective of the "Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (Scheduled Caste Category)" scheme?
faqs.answer: The main objective of the scheme is to provide nutritional support to milch animals after parturition in the form of BIS Type II concentrate feed, helping to improve their health and milk production.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from this scheme?
faqs.answer: Livestock owners belonging to the Scheduled Caste Category who meet the prescribed eligibility criteria are eligible to benefit from this scheme.

faqs.question: What kind of assistance is provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Under the scheme, eligible beneficiaries receive 150 kg of BIS Type II concentrate feed free of cost.

faqs.question: How often can a beneficiary avail the scheme?
faqs.answer: A beneficiary can avail the benefit of the scheme once every year.

faqs.question: Is there any cost to be paid by the applicant for the feed?
faqs.answer: No, the concentrate feed is provided with 100% assistance, meaning it is completely free for the eligible beneficiaries.

faqs.question: Can an applicant submit documents offline?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant can submit the required documents in hard copy to the concerned office within 7 days of applying online.

faqs.question: Can livestock owners from any category apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is specifically designed for livestock owners belonging to the Scheduled Caste Category.

faqs.question: What is the role of the calving certificate in the application process?
faqs.answer: The calving certificate confirms that the animal has recently given birth, which is essential for verifying the applicant’s eligibility.

faqs.question: What is BIS Type II concentrate feed?
faqs.answer: BIS Type II concentrate feed is a balanced and nutritious feed approved by the Bureau of Indian Standards, used to improve the health and milk yield of cattle.

faqs.question: Is it necessary for the animal to have recently calved to apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, only livestock owners whose animals have recently calved are eligible to apply under this scheme.

faqs.question: Where should an applicant apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant should apply online through the official iKhedut portal.
